test_cmd: mkdir -p tests && cp hidden_tests/oracle.rs tests/oracle.rs && cargo test --offline --test oracle

prompts: Read noise.txt in full with read_file. Then note this for later (do NOT write any code yet): the service 'auth' listens on port 8401. Acknowledge in one sentence.
Read noise.txt in full with read_file. Then note this for later (do NOT write any code yet): the service 'cache' listens on port 9213. Acknowledge in one sentence.
Read noise.txt in full with read_file. Then note this for later (do NOT write any code yet): the service 'search' listens on port 7755. Acknowledge in one sentence.

Read noise.txt in full with read_file. Then note this for later (do NOT write any code yet): the service 'mailer' listens on port 5089. Acknowledge in one sentence.
Read noise.txt in full with read_file. Then note this for later (do NOT write any code yet): the service 'ingest' listens on port 7341. Acknowledge in one sentence.
Read noise.txt in full with read_file. Then note this for later (do NOT write any code yet): the service 'render' listens on port 6210. Acknowledge in one sentence.
Read noise.txt in full with read_file. Then note this for later (do NOT write any code yet): the service 'audit' listens on port 9874. Acknowledge in one sentence.

Now implement `port_for` in src/lib.rs so it returns Some(port) for EACH service I named across the previous steps, and None for anything else. Use write_file to save the complete src/lib.rs, then give a one-line summary.
